author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
jugala⋅caraṇa⋅prati, parama⋅ānanda⋅tati,
ratipremā hou parabandhe
kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅rādhānāma, upāsanā rasadhāma,
caraṇe paḍiye parānande

manera smaraṇa prāṇa, madhura madhura nāma,
vilāsa jugala smṛtisāra
sādhya sādhana ei, āra nāi ihā boi,
ei tattva sarvatattva⋅sāra

jalada sundara⋅kānti, madhura madhura bhāti,
vaidagadhi⋅avadhi suveśa
supīta vasana dhara, ābharaṇa maṇivara,
mayūra⋅candrakā karu keśa

mṛgamada sucandana, kuṅkumādi vilepana,
mugdhakārī mūrati tribhaṅga
navīna⋅kusumāvalī, śrīaṅge śobhaye bhāli,
madhulobhe phire matta bhṛṅga

īṣat madhurasmita, vaidagadhi⋅līlāmṛta,
lubadhala vrajavadhūvṛnda
caraṇa⋅kamala⋅para, maṇimaya sumañjīra,
nakhamaṇi jini’ bālacandra

nūpura⋅marāla⋅dhvani, kulavadhū⋅marālinī,
śuniyā rahite nāre ghare
hṛdaye bāḍhaye rati, jena mile pati satī,
kulera dharama jāya dūre

kṛṣṇamukha⋅dvijarāje, saralā vaṁśī virāje,
jāra dhvani bhuvana mātāya
śravaṇera patha diyā, hṛdaye praveśa hañā,
prāṇa ādi ākarṣi’ ānaya

govinda⋅sevana satya, tā̐hāra sevaka nitya,
vṛndāvana bhūmi tejomaya
tāhāre jamunājala, kare nitya jhalamala,
tāra tīre aṣṭa kuñja haya

śītala kiraṇa kara, kalpataru⋅guṇadhara,
tarulatā ṣaḍṛtu⋅sevā
pūrṇacandrasama jyoti, cidānandamaya mūrti,
mahānanda daraśana lobhā

govinda ānandamaya, nikaṭe vanitācaya,
vihare madhura ati śobhā
du̐hu preme ḍagamagi, du̐he dohā anurāgī,
du̐hu rūpe du̐hu manolobhā

vrajapura⋅vanitāra, caraṇa āśraya sāra,
kara mana ekānta kariyā
anya bola gaṇḍagola, nāhi śuna utarola,
rākha prema hṛdaye bhariyā

kṛṣṇa prabhu ekabāra, karibena aṅgīkāra,
jena’ mana e satya vacana
dhanya līlā vṛndāvana, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa śrī⋅caraṇa,
dhanya sakhī mañjarīra gaṇa

pāpa⋅puṇyamaya deha, sakala anitya eha’,
dhana jana saba michā dhanda
marile jāibe kothā, tāhāte nā pāo vyathā,
tabu kārya kara sadā manda

rājāra je rājyapāṭa, jena nāṭuyāra nāṭa,
dekhite dekhite kichu naya
hena māyā kare jei, parama īśvara sei,
tā̐re mana sadā kara bhaya

pāpe nā kariha mana, adhama se pāpijana,
tāre mana dūre parihari’
puṇya se sukhera dhāma, tāra nā loio nāma,
‘puṇya’, ‘mukti’ dui tyāga kari’

premabhakti⋅sudhānidhi, tāhe ḍuba niravadhi,
āra jata kṣāranidhi prāya
nirantara sukha pābe, sakala santāpa jābe,
paratattva karile upāya

anyera paraśa jena, nāhi haya kadācana,
ihāte hoibe sāvadhāna
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa nāma gāna, ei se parama dhyāna,
āra nā kariha paramāṇa

karmī, jñānī, michābhakta, nā ha’be tāya anurakta,
śuddhabhajanete kara mana
vraja⋅janera jei mata, tāhe habe anugata,
ei se parama tattvadhana

prārthanā kariba sadā, śuddhabhāve premakathā,
nāma⋅mantre kariyā abheda
āstika kariyā mana, bhaja rāṅgā śrī⋅caraṇa,
granthipāpa habe pariccheda

rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅śrī⋅caraṇa, mātra paramārtha⋅dhana,
sayatane hṛdayete loo
du̐hu nāma śuni’ śuni’, bhaktamukhe puni puni,
parama ānanda sukha pāṅa

hemagauratanu rāi, ā̐khi daraśana cāi,
rodana karaye abhilāṣe

jaladhara ḍhara ḍhara, aṅga ati manohara,
rūpete bhuvana parakāśe

sakhīgaṇa cāripāśe, sevā kare abhilāṣe,
parama se śobhāsukha dhare
ei mane āśā mora, aiche rase hañā bhora,
narottama sadāi vihare
en_translation:
May my devotion and love be always bound to the feet of the Divine Couple, the highest happiness. The names of Kṛṣṇa and Rādhā are the abode of devotional mellows; I fall at Their feet in supreme bliss.

The remembrance of the mind is life's most precious possession; sweet are Their names—the essence of the Divine Couple's playful pastimes. This is the means and the goal; there is nothing beyond this truth, which is the essence of all truths.

With a beautiful cloud-like complexion and sweet radiance, Kṛṣṇa is adorned in beautiful garments with supreme artistry. He wears brilliant yellow clothes and the best of jewels, and His hair is decorated with peacock feathers.

Scented with musk, sandalwood paste, and vermilion, His enchanting form is bent in three places. Adorned with fresh flower garlands that beautify His divine body, intoxicated bees hover about Him, greedy for honey.

A slight, sweet smile—the nectar of His expert divine pastimes—enchants the damsels of Vraja. Upon His lotus feet are jeweled ankle bells, and His fingernails are brighter than the crescent moon.

Hearing the sound of His ankle bells, which is like the sound of swans, the chaste village women—like female swans themselves—cannot remain in their homes. Devotion grows in their hearts, as if husband and wife are meeting, and all worldly duties are cast aside.

On Kṛṣṇa's moon-like face, a simple flute shines, its sound intoxicating the universe. Entering through the path of the ears and into the heart, it attracts and brings forth the very life force.

Serving Govinda is the truth, and His servants are eternal. The land of Vṛndāvana is effulgent, and the waters of the Yamunā always sparkle there; on its banks are eight groves.

Cool rays of light shine there, and the trees and creepers possess the qualities of wish-fulfilling trees, offering service through the six seasons. His form is full of spiritual bliss and luminous like the full moon; I long to behold that great bliss.

Govinda is full of joy, surrounded by groups of women; His pastimes are sweet and exceedingly beautiful. Both are overflowing with love and devoted to each other, captivated by each other's beauty.

The shelter of the feet of the women of Vraja is the essence; make your mind exclusive to them. Do not listen to other clamors and disturbances; keep love filled in your heart.

Kṛṣṇa, the Master, will accept you; know this, O mind, for it is a true statement. Glorious are the pastimes of Vṛndāvana and the sacred feet of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa! Glorious are the companions and their attendants!

This body, full of sin and piety, everything is temporary; wealth and people are all false illusions. Where will you go after death? You feel no pain about it, yet you always do evil deeds.

The kingdom of a king is like an actor's play; while you watch it, it becomes nothing. He who creates such illusion is the Supreme Lord; fear Him always, O mind.

Do not set your mind on sin, for those who are sinful are degraded; cast them far away from your mind. Piety is the abode of happiness, but do not even utter its name; abandon both "piety" and "liberation."

Dive constantly into the nectar-ocean of loving devotion; all other oceans are like salty water. You will attain continuous happiness and all your distress will vanish if you make the Supreme Truth your means.

May there never be contact with those who are not devotees; be careful in this regard. Singing the names of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa is the supreme meditation; do not do anything else, even a tiny bit.

Do not be attached to fruitive workers, speculative philosophers, or false devotees; fix your mind on pure devotion. Follow the path of the people of Vraja; this is the supreme treasure of truth.

Always pray for pure loving talks, considering the Name and the mantra as non-different from God. With a faithful mind, worship the beautiful red feet, and the knot of sin will be severed.

The sacred feet of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa alone are the ultimate treasure; take them carefully into your heart. Hearing Their names again and again from the mouths of devotees, one attains supreme bliss and happiness.

I desire to see the golden-complexioned Rādhā; I cry with longing.

Like a fresh cloud, His form is most captivating, and His beauty illuminates the universe.

The companions around her serve her with longing, holding that supreme beauty and happiness. This is my heart's hope; immersed in such mellows, Narottama always sports.
